ranked_movies.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily discussed for its record-breaking box office opening, described as the 'Endgame' for horror fans and the Conjuring universe.

ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned for its iconic ending, practical effects, and as a benchmark for horror, cited in multiple threads including first theater experiences.

ranked_movies.title: Halloween
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently praised for its classic status, definitive slasher elements, and perfect ending. The original is a recurring point of reference.

ranked_movies.title: The Ring
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a generation-defining film that terrified audiences, with a profound cultural impact and one of the best theater experiences.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Widely discussed for its intense, mind-fucking horror and specific, unforgettable scenes that left a lasting impact on viewers.

ranked_movies.title: The Exorcist
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a timeless classic that still terrifies audiences decades later, with specific quotes and scenes mentioned as profoundly scary.

ranked_movies.title: The Descent
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended for its claustrophobic atmosphere, brutal ending, and as one of the best horror films of the 21st century.

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its terrifying quotes, Sam Neill's performance, and being a cult favorite that is often compared to 'Dead Space'.

ranked_movies.title: It Follows
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its unique, anxiety-inducing concept, brilliant soundtrack, and being a 'comfort horror' film for many fans.

ranked_movies.title: Signs
ranked_movies.reason: Specific scene ('Brazilian Birthday Party') is iconic and repeatedly mentioned as one of the most effective and memorable reveals in horror history.

ranked_movies.title: The Wailing
ranked_movies.reason: Hailed as a phenomenal Korean horror film with a complex story and deeply unsettling moments, often listed among the best international horrors.

ranked_movies.title: Hellraiser
ranked_movies.reason: Specifically mentioned for its terrifying quotes ('Jesus wept') and the profound impact of its imagery and tone.

ranked_movies.title: Alien
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its timeless scares and the plausibility of its premise, making it a foundational sci-fi horror film.

ranked_movies.title: 28 Days Later
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a definitive British horror that reinvented the zombie genre and is packed with intense, anxiety-fueled scenes.

ranked_movies.title: Drag Me to Hell
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed for its brutally unfair and memorable ending, which stands out for its bleakness and impact.

ranked_movies.title: Weapons
ranked_movies.reason: A recent release heavily discussed for its unique format, effective scares, and specific creepy scenes, compared favorably to 'Barbarian'.

ranked_movies.title: The Blair Witch Project
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned for its groundbreaking marketing, found-footage style, and being a terrifying first-time theater experience for many.

ranked_movies.title: The Witch
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its unsettling atmosphere and considered by many to be Robert Eggers' best work, a standout in modern horror.

ranked_movies.title: Sinister
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its effective soundtrack and ability to induce goosebumps, often listed as a top scary movie.

ranked_movies.title: Invasion of the Body Snatchers
ranked_movies.reason: The 1978 version is specifically highlighted for its amazing and brutally hopeless ending.
